_meta.description: Baseline test procedures for NACSA Act 854 CoP compliance audits
_meta.auditStandard: Directive on Audit Standards and Frequency (Direction No. 8)

testTypes.ToD: Test of Design — Assesses whether the control is properly designed to meet its objective
testTypes.ToE: Test of Effectiveness — Assesses whether the control operated effectively throughout the audit period

testProcedures.objective: Verify NCII entity registration with NACSA is complete and current
testProcedures.procedure: 1. Obtain documentation for NCII Entity Registration. 2. Review policy/procedure for completeness against Act 854 s17, s18 requirements. 3. Verify document is approved by appropriate authority within the past 12 months. 4. Confirm alignment with NACSA directives and sector CoP requirements. 5. Assess whether the control design addresses all key activities: Respond to NCII designation notice within the statutory period specified under Section 17; Appoint and register a primary and alternate NCII liaison officer with NACSA.
testProcedures.population: Governance documentation and organizational records
testProcedures.sampleSize: N/A — full population review
testProcedures.expectedResult: NCII Entity Registration is documented, current, approved, and aligned with Act 854 requirements

ratingCriteria.compliant: Documentation exists, is current (within 12 months), approved by appropriate authority, and covers all CoP requirements for s17, s18
ratingCriteria.partiallyCompliant: Documentation exists but is outdated, lacks formal approval, or has coverage gaps
ratingCriteria.nonCompliant: No documentation exists or documentation is fundamentally inadequate

testProcedures.objective: Test that NCII entity registration with NACSA is complete and current
testProcedures.procedure: 1. Obtain evidence of NCII Entity Registration operation for the audit period. 2. Select sample per sampling guide based on control frequency and population size. 3. For each sample item, verify the control operated as designed and documented. 4. Test for exceptions and evaluate their impact. 5. Verify management oversight and periodic reviews conducted. 6. Evaluate compensating controls for any deviations found.
testProcedures.population: Operational records and logs for NCII Entity Registration
testProcedures.sampleSize: Per sampling guide based on control frequency
testProcedures.expectedResult: NCII Entity Registration control operated effectively throughout the audit period

ratingCriteria.compliant: Control operated consistently with zero or one minor deviation; management oversight evidenced
ratingCriteria.partiallyCompliant: Control generally operated but with notable deviations or oversight gaps
ratingCriteria.nonCompliant: Control did not operate effectively; systematic deviations or no evidence of operation

testProcedures.objective: Test that IAM controls are designed for all NCII systems with least privilege
testProcedures.procedure: 1. Obtain population of ncii identity & access management records for the audit period. 2. Select sample per sampling guide. 3. For each sample item, verify control operated as designed: access requests approved, provisioned correctly, and reviewed. 4. Select sample per sampling guide based on control frequency and population size. 3. For each sample item, verify the control operated as designed and documented. 4. Test for exceptions and evaluate their impact. 5. Verify management oversight and periodic reviews conducted. 6. Evaluate compensating controls for any deviations found.
testProcedures.population: Operational records and logs for NCII Physical Monitoring & Surveillance
testProcedures.sampleSize: Per sampling guide based on control frequency
testProcedures.expectedResult: NCII Physical Monitoring & Surveillance control operated effectively throughout the audit period

ratingCriteria.compliant: Control operated consistently with zero or one minor deviation; management oversight evidenced
ratingCriteria.partiallyCompliant: Control generally operated but with notable deviations or oversight gaps
ratingCriteria.nonCompliant: Control did not operate effectively; systematic deviations or no evidence of operation

testProcedures.objective: Verify cloud governance framework addresses data sovereignty and shared responsibility
testProcedures.procedure: 1. Obtain NCII Cloud Security Governance documentation and supporting evidence. 2. Review control design for completeness against Act 854 s22, s23, s47 requirements. 3. Verify the control addresses relevant CoP domain requirements. 4. Assess whether control design covers key activities: Define a cloud security policy specifying requirements for NCII workload deployment including approved providers and service models; Assess data sovereignty and residency requirements to ensure NCII data remains within jurisdictional boundaries as required. 5. Evaluate control documentation for clarity, currency, and approval status.
testProcedures.population: NCII Cloud Security Governance documentation and configuration records
testProcedures.sampleSize: N/A — design assessment of documented controls
testProcedures.expectedResult: NCII Cloud Security Governance control is properly designed and documented to meet Act 854 requirements

ratingCriteria.compliant: Control is designed, documented, current, and addresses all relevant CoP requirements for s22, s23, s47
ratingCriteria.partiallyCompliant: Control exists but design has gaps, documentation is outdated, or coverage is incomplete
ratingCriteria.nonCompliant: Control is absent or design is fundamentally inadequate
